name: nock (Node.js HTTP Server Mocking)

agent_readiness.notes: Node.js HTTP request interceptor. nock('https://api.example.com').get('/users').reply(200, [{id:1}]). Intercepts Node.js http/https module. nock.cleanAll() to reset. .times(n) for repeated mocks. .delay(ms) for latency simulation. .matchHeader() for header assertions. afterEach(() => nock.cleanAll()). Works with axios, node-fetch, got, supertest. Pairs with Jest/Mocha. nock.recorder.rec() to record real HTTP for replay. Cannot mock browser fetch (use MSW).

gotchas: Node.js only (not browser) — use MSW for browser
Patches Node.js http module globally (can cause test isolation issues)
nock.cleanAll() required in afterEach to avoid test pollution
Does not work with fetch API in Node.js 18+ without shim
best_when: Node.js unit testing of code that makes HTTP calls
Mocking third-party APIs in Jest/Mocha Node.js tests
Express/Fastify integration tests with outbound HTTP mocking
avoid_when: When MSW provides better handler sharing across test environments
Projects using native fetch (Node 18+) without http compatibility layer
summary: nock intercepts Node.js HTTP/HTTPS requests for unit testing. nock('https://api.com').get('/path').reply(200, data). Works with axios, node-fetch, got. nock.recorder.rec() records real HTTP for replay. Pairs with Jest/Mocha. Node.js only (MSW for browser). nock.cleanAll() in afterEach for test isolation. The classic Node.js HTTP mocking solution.
